title: Muscle Building - Lean Bulking Program
description: A controlled caloric surplus designed to build muscle while minimizing fat gain. Ideal for those wanting better body composition.
overview: Lean bulking involves consuming a smaller surplus (200-300 calories) compared to aggressive bulking. This approach reduces fat gain significantly while still building muscle. Expected gains: 0.25-0.5 kg per week with mostly lean mass.
goals: Build muscle with minimal fat gain

weekly_schedule.exercises: Bench Press (4x6), Incline Dumbbell Press (3x8), Lateral Raises (3x12), Machine Chest Press (3x10), Cable Flyes (3x12)
Weighted Pull-ups (4x6), Barbell Rows (4x6), Lat Pulldowns (3x8), Seal Rows (3x10), Face Pulls (3x15)
Squats (4x6), Romanian Deadlifts (3x8), Leg Press (3x10), Leg Curls (3x12), Bulgarian Split Squats (3x8)
Barbell Curls (3x8), Close-grip Bench Press (3x8), Machine Rows (3x10), Tricep Rope Extensions (3x12), Hammer Curls (3x10)
Incline Bench (4x5), Pendulum Rows (4x6), Overhead Press (3x6), Machine Assisted Dips (3x8)

nutrition.content: 3 whole eggs + 1 slice whole wheat toast + ½ banana + 1 tbsp almond butter
200g grilled chicken + 150g brown rice + 120g broccoli + 1 tbsp olive oil
40g whey protein + 40g simple carbs (rice cakes or white rice)
200g lean beef or salmon + 150g sweet potato + 150g asparagus

tips: Use a food scale to measure portions accurately
Take weekly progress photos and measurements
Perform cardio 2-3 times per week for cardiovascular health
Adjust calories based on weekly weigh-ins - aim for 0.25-0.5 kg gain
Prioritize recovery with 7-9 hours of sleep
Stay consistent with training and nutrition
Focus on whole foods rather than processed options
Use mirrors and body composition assessment, not just the scale
